notes_md:
**cao** = tall or high. It describes height or level.

Use it for people, buildings, mountains, trees, and also for numbers like prices, temperatures, or scores. It comes after the noun: *người cao*, *nhà cao*, *giá cao*.  

It can also mean “high” in a more abstract way (like high cost or high level).  

Opposite: **thấp** (short, low).
exampleSentences: Anh của em rất cao nên luôn đứng ở phía sau khi chụp ảnh.
Chị muốn mua đôi giày này vì nó làm chị trông cao hơn.
Cây cột đèn ngoài đường này khá cao so với những cái khác.
notes:
cao = tall or high. It describes height or level.

Use it for people, buildings, mountains, trees, and also for numbers like prices, temperatures, or scores. It comes after the noun: người cao, nhà cao, giá cao.  

It can also mean “high” in a more abstract way (like high cost or high level).  

Opposite: thấp (short, low).

definitions.en: Having a greater than average height.
definitions.es: Tener una altura mayor que la media.
definitions.vi: Có chiều cao lớn hơn mức trung bình.

exampleSentencesTranslated: My older brother is very tall so he always stands at the back when taking photos.
I want to buy these shoes because they make me look tall.
This streetlight pole is quite tall compared to the others.
